revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)}}

It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)}}

It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|12=draft|12=draft}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)}}

It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]]

Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} * {{ill|Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]]

It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} * {{ill|Shugendō|lt=Shugendō|lt=Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|qq=}}, in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during {{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with {{ill|Shugendō|lt=Shugendō|lt=Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}, a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}}, separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁才天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} * {{ill|Shugendō|lt=Shugendō|lt=Shugendo|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendo|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]]

The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]], a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine underwent [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]]

It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]], a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine underwent [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]]

It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:福島県指定重要文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:木幡山・隠津島神社.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], Kihata Oaza Jike 49, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|37|19.9|N|140|34|37.6|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE, traditional) |Main Hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri with copper roof |alternate_name = Kihata no Benten-sama (弁天様) |Main Festival = April 25 |Rituals = Kihata's Banner Festival (December, first Sunday) |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima]], in the Kihata area. It is often referred to as "Kihata no Benten-sama" (木幡の弁天様) and is renowned for the annual Kihata's Banner Festival, designated as a [[Japan|Japanese]] [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. The shrine is situated on {{ill|Mount Kihata|ja|木幡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kihata|qq=}}, considered a sacred mountain. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}: * {{ill|Okitsushima-hime|ja|隠津島姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsushima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} The name "Kihata" (蚕機) is thought to symbolize silkworm cultivation, associating the shrine with the deity of sericulture.{{Cite book |title=日本の神々 |author=Katsuhiko Kikuchi |publisher=Hakusuisha |year=1984}} == History == Okitsushima Shrine is traditionally said to have been founded during [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 3 (769 CE) by {{ill|Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|ja|丈部継足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasetsukabe no Tsugutari|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Abe family. It is believed to have originated as a sacred site on Mount Kihata, later becoming associated with [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] and Buddhist practices.{{Cite book |title=式内社調査報告 |author=Yoichi Sato |volume=14 |publisher=皇學館大学出版部 |year=1986}} The shrine’s historical name, "Benten Shrine," reflects its association with [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]], a deity of wealth and prosperity. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine underwent [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]], separating its syncretic elements. It was renamed Okitsushima Shrine in 1902, aligning with its identification as a potential {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} shrine.{{Cite book |title=福島県の地名 |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1993}} == Festivals == The shrine holds its main festival on April 25 and the Kihata's Banner Festival in December. The latter, a unique ritual involving vibrant banners, has been preserved as a critical cultural heritage event.{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html |title=木幡の幡祭り |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == Architecture == The main hall, rebuilt in 1800 under the orders of the Nihonmatsu Domain, features a three-bay Nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The shrine complex also includes a historically significant {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}}, designated as a prefectural cultural property.{{Cite web |url=http://www4.pref.fukushima.jp/furusato-in/sub/shosai.php?data_id=601 |title=木幡山隠津島神社本殿、拝殿 |publisher=Fukushima Prefecture}} == Cultural Significance == The shrine encompasses Mount Kihata, a prefectural natural monument and health protection forest. It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:福島県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]]

It features notable relics, including a grand cedar designated a [[Natural Monument of Japan]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/history/bunkazai/kuni/100-290-001.html |title=木幡の大スギ |publisher=Nihonmatsu City}} == See Also == * [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] * [[:en:Benzaiten|Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendo]] == External Links == * [http://okitushima.com/ Official Website of Okitsushima Shrine] * [http://www.city.nihonmatsu.lg.jp/kanko/kohata/top.html Kihata's Banner Festival] (Nihonmatsu City) {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|version=102466617|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountain Worship]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Municipalities in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nihonmatsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nihonmatsu City]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Nihonmatsu)]] [[Category:二本松市の建築物]] [[Category:二本松市の歴史]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:福島県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]]
